---
title: "02_power_analysis_frequentist"
output: html_document
---

```{r}
# renv::install("simr")
library(simr)
library(lme4)
library(dplyr)

# 1. Create a dummy dataset for the power simulation
n_children <- 25
n_sessions <- 24

sim_data <- expand.grid(
  child_id = factor(1:n_children),
  session = 1:n_sessions,
  prep = factor(c("NoPrep", "Calming", "Stimulating", "ChildChoice")) # No spaces here!
)

# 2. Explicitly set levels so "NoPrep" is the Intercept (Better for interpretation)
sim_data$prep <- factor(sim_data$prep, 
                        levels = c("NoPrep", "Calming", "Stimulating", "ChildChoice"))

# 3. Define fixed effects - names must exactly match "factor_name" + "level_name"
fixed_effects <- c(
  "(Intercept)"    = 2.0, # This is now "NoPrep"
  "prepCalming"    = 0.2, # Calming vs NoPrep
  "prepStimulating" = 0.0, # Stimulating vs NoPrep
  "prepChildChoice" = 0.0  # ChildChoice vs NoPrep
)

# 3. Define Variances
# Random intercept variance (child differences)
rand_int_var <- 0.5 
# Residual variance (session noise)
res_var <- 0.8 

# 4. Initialize the 'fake' model for simr
freq_model <- makeLmer(
  engagement ~ prep  + (1 | child_id), 
  fixef = fixed_effects, 
  VarCorr = rand_int_var, 
  sigma = res_var, 
  data = sim_data
)
```

```{r}
# Power to detect the effect of 'prep' (the whole factor)
# This tests if there is ANY difference between the 4 prep types
power_prep <- powerSim(freq_model, fixed("prep", "f"), nsim = 100)
print(power_prep)
```

```{r}
# Calculate power for different numbers of sessions (8, 12, 16, 20, 24)
pc <- powerCurve(freq_model, fixed("prepChildChoice", "z"), 
                 along = "session", breaks = c(8, 12, 16, 20, 24))

plot(pc)
```

```{r}
library(simr)
library(lme4)

n_children <- 25
n_sessions <- 30

sim_data <- expand.grid(
  child_id = factor(1:n_children),
  session  = 1:n_sessions,
  prep     = factor(c("NoPrep", "Calming", "Stimulating", "ChildChoice"),
                    levels = c("NoPrep", "Calming", "Stimulating", "ChildChoice"))
)

sim_data$engagement <- NA_real_  # placeholder so formula parses

fixed_effects <- c("(Intercept)"     = 2.0,
                   "prepCalming"     = 0.1,
                   "prepStimulating" = 0.1,
                   "prepChildChoice" = 0.1)

rand_var <- list(
  child_id = matrix(c(0.5, 0, 0, 0,
                      0, 0.8, 0, 0,
                      0, 0, 0.8, 0,
                      0, 0, 0, 0.8),
                    nrow = 4)
)

individual_power_model <- makeLmer(
  engagement ~ prep + (1 + prep | child_id),
  fixef   = fixed_effects,
  VarCorr = rand_var,
  sigma   = 0.5,
  data    = sim_data
)

# ✅ FIX: replace NAs with one real simulated draw so fcompare() can fit
sim_data$engagement <- simulate(individual_power_model)[[1]]

# ✅ Rebuild model with populated data
individual_power_model <- makeLmer(
  engagement ~ prep + (1 + prep | child_id),
  fixef   = fixed_effects,
  VarCorr = rand_var,
  sigma   = 0.5,
  data    = sim_data
)

power_heterogeneity <- powerSim(
  individual_power_model,
  fcompare(~ 1 + (1 | child_id)),
  nsim = 50
)

print(power_heterogeneity)
```
